frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing Earth globe and introductory graphics. No task performance content yet.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content opening; no FLS task timing begins.

frame_analyses.description: Presenter identification: Todd Ponsky, MD, FACs, Pediatric Surgeon, Cincinnati Children's Hospital.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Expert credentials established. Video is instructional lecture by expert.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic equipment display: 3 or 5mm endocamera shown on blue surgical drape with connecting cables.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational equipment overview; narrated instruction context.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view of red foam organ model with multiple appendages visible. Single instrument (grasper/dissector) entering field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First appearance of task setup and working field. Appendages visible as pink/red foam structures.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing foam abdominal cavity. Text overlay: 'Step 1: Push the sac into the abdomen.' Instrument retracting/mobilizing tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional slide showing anatomical context (abdominal approach step). Not the actual endoloop task—this is demonstrating PPV closure technique context.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view of foam model. Text overlay: 'Step 2: Resect the sac.' Instrument manipulating tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional context for related procedure. Not endoloop task performance.

frame_analyses.description: Close-up laparoscopic view showing cord structures. Annotations: 'Cord vessels' and 'Vas deferens.' Text: 'If you're not sure, do NOT do it or do it partially.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Safety teaching: identifies critical structures (vas deferens, cord vessels) to avoid. Expert-level anatomical awareness training.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing tissue dissection with bleeding/coagulation evident. Text: 'Be careful with adjacent structures.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Emphasizes precision and avoiding collateral tissue trauma. Demonstrates consequence of careless dissection.

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Double Loop Technique' displayed on teal background.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transitions to instructional content on advanced ligating loop technique for complex cases.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing peritoneal dissection around cord structures. Text: 'This helps dissect the peritoneum to avoid the lesion of the cord structures.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Teaching advanced anatomy-aware dissection. Demonstrates peritoneal mobilization technique for safe ligating loop placement.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view with 18-gauge spinal needle visible. Text: '18 gauge spinal needle through half the IIO.' Needle passing through internal ring opening area on one side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates needle trajectory for double loop technique. Ipsilateral placement shown first to establish anatomy.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view. Text: 'Pass the needle through the contralateral side with a new prolene loop.' Needle and suture material crossing midline.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows contralateral passage technique. Demonstrates needle driver manipulation and loop positioning across anatomy.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing two crossing suture lines/loops. Text: 'Use the first loop as a snare to take out the second one.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Advanced technique: using first loop as instrument control mechanism for second loop. Demonstrates surgical problem-solving.

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Contralateral side. Closing a Small PPV like this has been contested.' Teal background.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Discusses clinical controversy in surgical technique. Educational context acknowledges debate in field.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing needle passing through internal ring opening on one side. Text: 'Pass an 18 gauge spinal needle through one side of the IIO.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates first needle placement for contralateral approach. Shows anatomical landmark (internal ring opening).

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing needle passed through tissue with loop material visible inside previously placed loop. Text: 'Pass the needle inside the loop previously inserted.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates sequential loop placement. Shows loop-within-loop technique for complex anatomy.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing completion of second loop trajectory through tissue. Text: 'Step 7: Use the first loop as a snare and take the second loop through the other side.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows final step of double loop technique. Demonstrates loop-through-loop manipulation for securing tissue.

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Let's summarize!' on teal background.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transitions to summary section of instructional content.

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Be careful with the cord structures' displayed on teal background with white text emphasis.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final reinforcement of critical safety point. Emphasizes anatomical awareness as paramount.

frame_analyses.description: Contact information slide showing YouTube, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and LinkedIn handles for 'Stay Current MD' educator.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content closure with instructor branding.

score_components.formula_applied: Not applicable—instructional content, not a performance evaluation

confidence_rationale: This is clearly instructional/educational content with narration, title slides, presenter identification, and teaching context throughout. No trainee or surgeon is performing the FLS endoloop task; instead, an expert (Todd Ponsky, MD) is demonstrating and explaining advanced surgical technique (double loop technique) for ligating loop placement in complex inguinal hernia repair scenarios. No performance scoring is warranted.
technique_summary: This is an expert-level educational lecture on the ligating loop (endoloop) task and advanced double loop technique for pediatric inguinal hernia repair. The content demonstrates expert anatomical knowledge, safety consciousness, and surgical problem-solving approaches.

demonstrated_techniques: Standard endoloop placement on foam appendage model
Double loop technique for complex inguinal hernia cases
Peritoneal dissection to isolate cord structures
Ipsilateral and contralateral needle passage through internal ring opening
verbal_cues: Push the sac into the abdomen (Step 1)
If you're not sure, do NOT do it or do it partially
This helps dissect the peritoneum to avoid the lesion of the cord structures
Pass the needle through the contralateral side with a new prolene loop
Use the first loop as a snare to take out the second one
common_errors_addressed: Damaging vas deferens or cord vessels during dissection—explicitly warned against
Incomplete peritoneal mobilization—teaching shows proper dissection plane
Improper needle trajectory—demonstrates correct passage through internal ring opening
Inadequate loop security on complex anatomy—double loop technique overcomes single-loop limitations
Hesitation or partial completion—instructor emphasizes commitment to technique or abandonment, not compromise
teaching_content.instructor_credentials: Todd Ponsky, MD, FACs, Pediatric Surgeon, Cincinnati Children's Hospital

video_classification_justification: This video is **instructional content** because it contains: (1) expert presenter with credentials identified; (2) title slides and section transitions with narrated context; (3) multiple educational segments on different surgical approaches; (4) safety teaching and anatomy warnings; (5) no continuous task performance by a trainee; (6) deliberate pauses and explanation of technique rationale; (7) closing with instructor branding and contact information. It is not a 'performance' video of a trainee attempting the FLS endoloop task, nor is it an 'expert_demo' showing a single expert completing the task for direct comparison—it is comprehensive surgical education on ligating loop technique and advanced variations.
